reasoning: The analysis identified f1_c_packet_loss_dl_percentage as the primary root cause indicator due to its low anomaly score. A control plane collapse on the F1-c interface (connecting O-CU and O-DU) logically precedes and triggers failures across other interfaces (F1-u, E2, A1) and components. The widespread nature of the anomaly is consistent with high packet loss on the F1-c interface, leading to synchronization and signaling failures.
actionable_steps:
1. Perform a physical layer check and link stability analysis on the transport network connecting the O-CU and O-DU.
2. Inspect O-CU and O-DU logs for F1-C setup/maintenance failures or SCTP association timeouts.
3. Verify if any recent configuration changes were applied to the control plane traffic shaping or QoS policies on the F1-c interface.
